Title: Evolution of TPS20-related terpene synthases influences chemical diversity in the glandular trichomes of the wild tomato relative Solanum habrochaites

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: volatile specialized metabolite; contributes to trichome chemical diversity and likely ecological interactions
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-e/f subfamily terpene synthase with plastid transit peptide and modified DDXXD/NSE-DTE motifs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-phellandrene; limonene; α-pinene; δ-2-carene; α-phellandrene; γ-terpinene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes NPP to a blend of monoterpenes dominated by β-phellandrene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: produces β-phellandrene-rich monoterpene profile matching LA2100 trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: homology-based cloning; phylogenetic analysis; codon-optimized heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with NPP; GC-MS comparison with trichome extracts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: volatile metabolite profiling by GC-MS; cDNA sequencing (GenBank JN990661–JN990694)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A TPS20-related allele from LA2100 encoded a monoterpene synthase designated ShPHS1. 2. Recombinant ShPHS1 used NPP, not reported trans-prenyl substrates, to produce a monoterpene blend dominated by β-phellandrene. 3. The in vitro products recapitulated the monoterpene chemistry observed in LA2100 trichomes, linking TPS20 allelic variation to terpene diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cluster M/clade A TPS20-related enzyme; monoterpene-producing accessions are geographically biased near the Peru-Ecuador border
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for engineering monoterpene-rich trichomes and altering volatile blends in tomato

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: volatile specialized metabolite; contributes to trichome chemical diversity and ecological interactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cloning from accession LA2812; heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay with NPP; GC-MS product identification; comparison with trichome extracts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: volatile metabolite profiling by GC-MS; cDNA sequencing (GenBank JN990661–JN990694)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A clade B TPS20-related enzyme from LA2812A was designated ShLMS. 2. Recombinant ShLMS converted NPP predominantly to limonene. 3. Its in vitro product profile matched the limonene-rich trichome profile of LA2812, demonstrating allelic functional divergence at the TPS20 locus.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth and in vitro assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents one of the previously unassigned TPS-e/f activities identified in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for engineering limonene-rich specialized metabolism in tomato trichomes

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: volatile specialized metabolite; contributes to trichome chemical diversity and ecological interactions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts NPP predominantly to α-pinene with additional β-pinene and β-phellandrene/limonene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes α-pinene and β-pinene accumulation in trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cloning from accession LA2107; heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay with NPP; GC-MS comparison with trichome extracts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: volatile metabolite profiling by GC-MS; cDNA sequencing (GenBank JN990661–JN990694)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A clade C TPS20-related allele from LA2107 encoded a new monoterpene synthase designated ShPIS. 2. Recombinant ShPIS used NPP to produce predominantly α-pinene plus β-pinene and minor monoterpenes. 3. High β-pinene levels in LA2107 trichomes were specifically explained by ShPIS activity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth and in vitro assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Trichomes of LA2107 also accumulate products attributable to ShPHS1-like activity, suggesting multiple alleles/paralogs or heterozygosity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful to engineer pinene-rich trichome volatiles

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: volatile specialized metabolites associated with trichome chemical diversity; possible defense-related ecological roles
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key determinant of module A terpene branch in accessions carrying the allele
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes module A sesquiterpene accumulation in group I accessions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: previous characterization plus recombinant expression of clade D enzyme from LA1393 in E. coli; assay with 2z,6z-FPP; GC-MS comparison with trichome extracts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: volatile metabolite profiling by GC-MS; phylogenetic analysis; accessioned sequences include ACJ38409/FJ194970
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Clade D TPS20-related enzymes encode ShSBS activity using 2z,6z-FPP. 2. Recombinant LA1393 enzyme produced the same sesquiterpene blend as previously characterized LA1777 ShSBS. 3. ShSBS products define terpene module A and are geographically restricted in central Peru.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Products may undergo further metabolism to corresponding acids, reducing volatile abundance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: geographic distribution/climatic context associated with ShSBS-containing accessions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Group S enzymes contain exon 4 region similar to TPS18, potentially linked to substrate specificity shift toward 2z,6z-FPP
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for breeding/engineering defensive sesquiterpene pathways in tomato, though restricted natural distribution suggests possible adaptation tradeoffs

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: known insect repellent; volatile defense metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 2z,6z-FPP to 7-epizingiberene with associated R-curcumene and β-sesquiphellandrene
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key determinant of module E sesquiterpene branch in accessions carrying the allele
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes high accumulation of 7-epizingiberene-rich sesquiterpene profile in trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cloning from LA2167; codon-optimized heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay with 2z,6z-FPP; GC-MS comparison with trichome extracts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: volatile metabolite profiling by GC-MS; cDNA sequencing (GenBank JN990661–JN990694)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A clade E TPS20-related enzyme from LA2167 was identified as ShZIS. 2. Recombinant ShZIS used 2z,6z-FPP to synthesize 7-epizingiberene, R-curcumene and β-sesquiphellandrene, corresponding to terpene module E. 3. This allele explains the dominant sesquiterpene chemistry of group II accessions and provides a gene for engineering insect-repellent 7-epizingiberene production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some accessions carry apparently functional ShZIS-like alleles yet lack 7-epizingiberene, implying dependence on substrate supply or gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: geographic distribution across Peru/Ecuador; greenhouse growth for profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Widely distributed compared with ShSBS; suggested to have originated earlier than ShSBS in evolution of TPS20-related enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high value target for breeding or engineering arthropod resistance in cultivated tomato and other crops via 7-epizingiberene production
